free_text: Neutron induced fission of 232-Th near threshold.
Abstract, no data
#NSR: 1981LAZW
Time-of-flight
Cf-252 source was used for calibration.
Ortec heavy-ion surface barrier detector.
Large spherical 232Th -66 cm2 and 490 microgms/cm2.
See Ref-Phys.Rev. C22,2485,1980.
Fragment masses were deduced from fragment energies
and fragment flight times.
Statistical uncertainties.
The pulse-height defect of the heavy-ion detector
was corrected by the method described in Ref-
H.W.Schmitt+,Phys.Rev.137B,837,1965.
Fission-fragment yield peak-to-valley ratio at 1.6,
respectively.
From Table I, Phys.Rev.C28,1212,1983(subs. 2-7)
and from author (sub. 8).

OG. 2nd reference added. STATUS updated.
#doi was added. #NSR was added.
The light and heavy fragment mean kinetic energies
were corrected from loss in the target.
The mean kinetic energies for the light fission
fragment group  were determined from the data by
excluding the events in the valley region due to
poor statistics.

Statistical uncertainty for kinetic energy= 100KeV.
Systematic uncertainty for kinetic energy = 1 MEV.
This main source of error in the energy determination
was due to the correction of pulse-height defect of
the heavy-ion detector.
